title: lithocarpus grandifrons
intro: Lithocarpus grandifrons, also known as the giant conker tree, is a species of evergreen tree that belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae). The bark of this tree has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Lithocarpus grandifrons have been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Lithocarpus grandifrons is often prepared as a decoction or tea by steeping the bark in hot water, and it can also be used in traditional herbal remedies such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments.
